context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the write-tests instructions from the published Rails Agent Skills tile.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill when the task is to write, review, or clean up RSpec tests.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Core principle:** Prefer behavioral confidence over implementation coupling. Good specs are readable, deterministic, and cheap to maintain.

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Write the failing spec** — pick the smallest spec type that exercises the intended behaviour (model > service > request > system).

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Run it and confirm the failure message** — the error should be about missing code, not a setup problem.

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify** — run the full relevant spec file, then the suite, before committing.

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Minimal factories only. Never rely on factory defaults for business logic — set explicitly or use traits. Avoid `create` when `build`/`build_stubbed` suffices.

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use only when the same behavioural contract applies to multiple subjects without per-example `let` overrides. Avoid when each context needs different setup — that signals a wrong abstraction.

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: When asked to write or review RSpec specs, your output MUST satisfy each rule below. Each is graded independently — one violation drops the whole check.

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **`let` for test data**, `let!` ONLY when the object must exist before the action under test.
